pillarScores.summary: Good foundational practices with encryption, but critical IAM policy gaps identified
Strong multi-AZ architecture with minor backup coverage gaps
Good caching implementation but lacks data-driven resource sizing
Strong cost governance and monitoring practices

issues.title: IAM Policies Contain Wildcard Permissions
issues.description: IAM policies contain wildcard permissions that violate the least privilege principle

riskAssessment.impact: Potential for privilege escalation and unauthorized access to AWS resources

steps: Review all IAM policies and identify wildcard permissions
Replace wildcards with specific resource ARNs
Implement separate roles for different application functions
Use IAM Access Analyzer to validate least privilege
Establish regular IAM policy review process

issues.title: Internal Service Communication Not Encrypted
issues.description: Internal service-to-service communication within the VPC is not encrypted

riskAssessment.impact: Potential for data interception within VPC

steps: Implement TLS for internal service communication
Use AWS Certificate Manager for certificate management
Configure application load balancers for internal services with HTTPS
Consider AWS App Mesh for service mesh with automatic TLS

issues.title: Application State Not Backed Up
issues.description: Application configuration and state stored in S3 are not included in backup strategy

riskAssessment.impact: Potential data loss for application configuration

steps: Enable S3 versioning for configuration buckets
Configure S3 replication to another region
Document backup and restore procedures
Define RTO and RPO requirements
Test recovery procedures regularly

issues.title: Instance Sizing Based on Estimates
issues.description: EC2 instance sizing is based on initial estimates rather than performance testing and actual workload analysis

riskAssessment.impact: Potential for over-provisioning (increased cost) or under-provisioning (poor performance)

steps: Conduct load testing to determine actual resource requirements
Monitor CPU, memory, and network utilization under realistic load
Use AWS Compute Optimizer recommendations
Implement auto-scaling based on performance metrics
Document instance sizing decisions and rationale

issues.title: Manual Resource Decommissioning
issues.description: Resource decommissioning relies on manual quarterly reviews, which is inefficient and may miss unused resources

riskAssessment.impact: Unused resources may accumulate, increasing costs

steps: Implement AWS Config rules to identify unused resources
Create Lambda functions to automatically tag resources for deletion
Use AWS Trusted Advisor recommendations
Set up automated notifications for idle resources
Establish automated cleanup policies for non-production environments

documentationGaps.title: IAM Policy Management Documentation
missingInformation: IAM policy review process and schedule
documentationGaps.recommendedAction: Create security operations runbook documenting IAM management procedures

documentationGaps.title: Resource Sizing Documentation
documentationGaps.recommendedAction: Document performance testing procedures and maintain sizing decision log

documentationGaps.title: Backup and Recovery Documentation
missingInformation: RTO (Recovery Time Objective) and RPO (Recovery Point Objective) requirements
documentationGaps.recommendedAction: Create disaster recovery runbook with documented RTO/RPO targets

strengths.title: Data Protection at Rest
strengths.description: All data encrypted using AWS KMS across RDS, S3, and EBS

strengths.title: Data Protection in Transit
strengths.description: TLS 1.2+ enforced for all external traffic with VPC endpoints

strengths.title: High Availability Architecture
strengths.description: Multi-AZ deployment with auto-scaling and load balancing

strengths.title: Multi-Layer Caching
strengths.description: CloudFront CDN and ElastiCache Redis for optimal performance

strengths.title: Cost Governance
strengths.description: AWS Budgets and cost allocation tags implemented

security: Implement IAM Access Analyzer for continuous policy validation
Consider AWS Security Hub for centralized security monitoring
reliability: Define and document RTO/RPO requirements for all critical systems
Consider multi-region deployment for critical components
performance-efficiency: Establish performance testing as part of CI/CD pipeline
Use AWS Compute Optimizer for ongoing instance recommendations
cost-optimization: Consider Reserved Instances or Savings Plans for predictable workloads
Review and optimize data transfer costs

nextSteps: Review findings with stakeholders and discuss priorities
Assign remediation tasks to appropriate teams
Create tracking tickets for each remediation item
Schedule follow-up review in 3 months to track progress
Update documentation to address identified gaps
Integrate Well-Architected reviews into development workflow
